config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because once individual rights can be overridden too easily, abuse becomes very likely. In my view, a healthy society protects personal freedom while limiting it only when there is a clear and necessary public reason. From my own perspective, people are strongest when they are treated as citizens with rights, not just as tools for the collective.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should sometimes take priority, but not as a blanket rule over individual rights. From my perspective as someone from China, I have seen how social stability and collective goals can matter a lot, especially in times of crisis, but I also know that when the individual is treated as expendable, abuse becomes easy to justify. The right balance is not “society first no matter what,” but rather “the common good first only when the restriction is necessary, proportionate, and accountable.” If individual rights are too weak, society may look orderly on the surface while becoming unjust underneath.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, I understand why people value harmony, public order, and communal responsibility, but I have also seen how easily “for the good of society” can become an excuse to silence criticism or mistreat minorities. The common good matters, but it must be limited by clear rights, because a society that ignores individual dignity may look stable while becoming unfair underneath. I would only accept restrictions on individual rights when they are truly necessary, proportional, and accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should sometimes take priority, but never as a free pass to override individual rights. From my perspective as someone from China, I do value social stability and the idea that people should contribute to the common good, especially in emergencies or when public order is at risk, but I have also seen how dangerous it is when “society first” becomes an excuse to silence criticism or ignore unfair treatment. A society is not truly strong if it only looks orderly on the surface while individual dignity is sacrificed underneath. The right standard is necessity, proportionality, and accountability, not blanket priority for either side.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should never be treated as an automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, communal values and social harmony are important, but I have seen how easily “public interest” can be used to justify censorship, discrimination, or pressure on minorities, and I do not trust that logic when it is unchecked. A society is healthier when it protects individual dignity first and only limits rights in narrow, necessary, and accountable cases. If the collective can override the person too easily, then the collective stops being just and starts becoming oppressive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should sometimes come before individual rights, but never as an automatic rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I do value social stability and collective responsibility, and I do not think a society can function well if everyone only thinks about themselves, especially in crises. But I disagree with the idea that the common good can be used too easily to silence criticism, weaken minorities, or excuse unfair treatment, because that creates order on the surface and injustice underneath. The only acceptable standard is that any limit on individual rights must be necessary, proportionate, and accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
